svg.text: • Web crawler — visit URLs in order of distance from seed.
• Social network "people you may know" within k hops.
• Shortest path in routing protocols on unweighted topologies (RIP hop counts).
• Image segmentation by flood fill / connected component labeling.
• Game AI — BFS over game state graph for shortest move sequence.
• GC tracing collectors — mark reachable from roots layer-by-layer.
• Cycle detection in concurrent systems / dependency analysis.
• Bitmap frontier — Pregel/Giraph use compact bitmaps for vertex-active state.
• Direction-optimizing BFS (Beamer 2012) — switch top-down ↔ bottom-up by frontier density.
• Graph500 benchmark — BFS on 2³³ vertices is the canonical HPC graph workload.
• Distributed BFS — Pregel-style supersteps; one BFS layer per superstep.
• GPU BFS — frontier-based with atomic queues; achieves 10–100x CPU on dense graphs.
• External BFS — semi-external memory algorithms for graphs that exceed RAM.
For social-graph distance: bidirectional BFS reduces frontier by √, often 100× faster.
